food.name: Regular Cola

identity.basisReason: Per-100g basis matches beverage-label conventions and keeps comparisons clean against other drinks.
excludes: cola with added juice or dairy

scoreReadiness.summary: This generic regular-cola record is simple and stable enough for launch scripting because the core story is just sugar delivery plus minimal nutrition.

sourceNotes: Identity locked to standard sugar-sweetened regular cola, per 100 g as sold.
Core nutrient values align with generic beverage nutrition-label style cola entries.
This is intentionally a generic regular-cola record rather than a specific brand SKU.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 10.6g of carbs. And that is basically the whole nutrition story.
sections.subtitleText: 10.6g of carbs. And that is basically the whole nutrition story.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: Positives first: very available and cheap. That is about the only practical argument it has. Use case can matter more than macros. This category is really about function and trade-offs. Sometimes makes sense in a narrow use case. Context matters more here than raw nutrient purity.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: Downsides next: liquid sugar is a brutal downside. This is almost the exact kind of misc item the category should bury. Basically no useful nutrition. It gives up nutritional value while still costing calories. Easy to oversell beyond what the numbers support. Misc items often attract exaggerated narratives.
